title: Nizam Work-Packet Schema
description: Validates a work-packet (templates/work-packet.template.json): the atomic, dispatchable unit of contracted work an execution ledger (for example a Kanban dispatcher) binds to a plan of record before implementation begins. The six required fields (id, objective, scope, acceptance, evidence, non_goals) are the historical minimal work-packet and keep every pre-existing packet valid. The optional linking/dispatch fields let a runtime dispatcher bind a packet to cross-repo governance -- its architecture tier (standard/cross_repo_governance.md seven-tier model), its upstream/downstream dependency edges and merge ordering (methodology/08_cross_repo_dependency_gate.md), and the contract/feature/phase/release-train records it belongs to -- without introducing vocabulary that conflicts with those two governance documents.

objective.description: The single, concrete outcome this work-packet must achieve.

scope.description: The explicit path boundary this work-packet authorizes work within.

allowed_paths.description: Repository-relative path globs the packet may create or modify.

forbidden_paths.description: Repository-relative path globs the packet must never touch.

acceptance.description: Human-readable acceptance criteria the packet must satisfy to be considered complete.

evidence.description: Paths to the externalised evidence files proving the acceptance criteria are met.

non_goals.description: Explicit statements of what this work-packet does NOT authorize.

repo.description: Optional. Identifier of the repository this work-packet executes in, for a dispatcher coordinating work across a multi-repository ecosystem (standard/cross_repo_governance.md).

tier.description: Optional. The executing repository's tier in the seven-tier architecture model (standard/cross_repo_governance.md Sec 3). Lower tiers are upstream; higher tiers depend on them. tier-0 Identity & Access; tier-1 Edge & Web Presence; tier-2 Gated Portals; tier-3 Governance & Strategy; tier-4 Runtime Services; tier-5 Infrastructure & Persistence; tier-6 Reference Archive & Legacy.

blast_radius.description: Optional. How far the packet's change can propagate, so a dispatcher can gate concurrency and review depth: 'local' (confined to the executing repository), 'cluster' (affects a group of same-tier or tightly-coupled repositories), 'ecosystem' (crosses tiers or alters a shared contract, engaging the cross-repo dependency gate of methodology/08_cross_repo_dependency_gate.md).

concurrency_lane.description: Optional. Dispatcher lane identifier used to serialise packets that must not run concurrently (for example packets touching the same shared contract) while allowing packets in different lanes to proceed in parallel.

dependency_edges.description: Optional. This packet's edges in the ecosystem dependency DAG (methodology/08_cross_repo_dependency_gate.md). A dispatcher uses these to block a packet until its upstream deltas are approved and to notify downstream consumers.

depends_on.description: Identifiers of upstream work-packets, contracts, or repositories whose contract deltas must be approved before this packet may execute.

consumed_by.description: Identifiers of downstream work-packets, contracts, or repositories that depend on this packet's output.

contract_id.description: Optional. Foreign key to the .agent/contracts/NNN.json contract this packet implements (schema/contract.schema.json 'contract_id').

phase_id.description: Optional. Foreign key to the phase this packet belongs to (schema/phase.schema.json 'id').

feature_id.description: Optional. Foreign key to the feature this packet delivers (schema/feature_list.schema.json feature 'id').

train_id.description: Optional. Foreign key to the release train this packet is scheduled into (methodology/06_release_train.md).

merge_order.description: Optional. The packet's position in its train's merge sequence, honouring the upstream-before-downstream ordering of the cross-repo dependency gate (methodology/08_cross_repo_dependency_gate.md). Lower merges first.
